profile.education: Dartmouth College (BA, History); Harvard Law School (JD)
profile.background: Attorney; North Carolina state senator (2009-2016); Attorney General (2017-2025); became 76th governor and first Jewish governor of North Carolina in 2025.

candidates.name: Vi Lyles
candidates.notes: Re-elected to 5th consecutive term Nov 2025. Democrat. Longest-serving current Charlotte mayor.

candidates.name: James Mitchell Jr.
candidates.office: City Council, At-Large / Mayor Pro Tem
candidates.notes: Elected Mayor Pro Tem Dec 2025. At-Large council member. Democrat.

candidates.name: Dimple Ajmera
candidates.notes: At-Large council member. Democrat. Led at-large vote with 21.04%.

candidates.name: Victoria Watlington
candidates.notes: At-Large council member. Democrat. Made allegations of unethical activities within council.

candidates.name: LaWana Slack-Mayfield
candidates.notes: At-Large council member. Democrat.

candidates.name: Dante Anderson
candidates.notes: Charlotte City Council District 1. Democrat.

candidates.name: Malcolm Graham
candidates.notes: Charlotte City Council District 2. Democrat. Former state senator.

candidates.name: Joi Mayo
candidates.notes: Newly sworn in Dec 2025. Charlotte City Council District 3. Democrat.

candidates.name: Renee Perkins Johnson
candidates.notes: Charlotte City Council District 4. Democrat.

candidates.name: JD Mazuera Arias
candidates.notes: Newly sworn in Dec 2025. Won extremely close race (50.3%). Charlotte City Council District 5. Democrat.

candidates.name: Kimberly Owens
candidates.notes: Newly sworn in Dec 2025. First Democrat to represent District 6 (south Charlotte). Defeated Republican Krista Bokhari.

candidates.name: Ed Driggs
candidates.notes: Charlotte City Council District 7. Only Republican on council. Ran unopposed 2025.

candidates.name: Spencer Merriweather
candidates.notes: Incumbent DA running for re-election Nov 2026. Democrat. Advanced from Democratic primary unopposed. Covers Charlotte/Mecklenburg County.

candidates.name: Janet Cowell
candidates.notes: Elected 2024 as Raleigh's first new mayor in years. Former NC State Treasurer (2009-2016). Democrat. Nonpartisan office. Running for re-election 2026.

candidates.name: Lorrin Freeman
candidates.notes: Current Wake County DA. Democrat. First elected 2014. Not seeking re-election; term ends Dec 2026. 2026 Democratic primary has three candidates: Wiley Nickel (former US Rep), Melanie Shekita, Sherita Walton. No Republican filed.
